IsA: It is a pair of six-panel folding-screens.
It is a seminal work in the development of Japanese folding screens.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by being a meticulously detailed birds-eye view of a fortress and its nearby town.
UsedFor: It is used for depicting Azuchi Castle and its nearby town.
It is used for display in a gallery of the Vatican.
It is used for decoration of a room in a palace.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Vatican collections.
It is located in a gallery of the Vatican.
It is located in the Galleria delle carte geografiche.
It is located in the gallery of Cosmography.
It is located in Eggenberg Palace in Graz, Austria.
It is located in a room of the palace.
CreatedBy: It was created by Kanō Eitoku.
CreatedIn: It was created in Azuchi Castle.
PublishedIn: It was published in Second Part of the Images of Indian Gods.
It was published in Images of the Gods and Ancients.
ProducedBy: It was produced by Filippo Ferroverde.
ConsistsOf: It consists of depictions of Azuchi Castle and its nearby town.
PartOf: It is part of the Vatican collections.
It is part of the Tenshō Embassy.
It is part of a gallery of the Vatican.
It is part of the Galleria delle carte geografiche.
It is part of the gallery of Cosmography.
It is part of Eggenberg Palace in Graz, Austria.
It is part of a room of the palace.
KnownFor: It is known for being seminal works in the development of Japanese folding screens.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Oda Nobunaga.
It is associated with Alessandro Valignano.
It is associated with Pope Gregory XIII.
It is associated with the Tenshō Embassy.
It is associated with Kanō Eitoku.
It is associated with Philips van Winghe.
It is associated with Filippo Ferroverde.
It is associated with Lorenzo Pignoria.
It is associated with Vincent Catari.
It is associated with the Azuchi Screens Research Network.
NamedAfter: It is named after Azuchi Castle.
HeldIn: It was held in the Vatican collections.
It was held in a gallery of the Vatican.
It was held in the Galleria delle carte geografiche.
It was held in the gallery of Cosmography.
About: It is about the nearby town of Azuchi Castle.
Influenced: It influenced the development of Japanese folding screens.
